sourceMovie.title: The Conjuring
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Conjuring (2013) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. James Wan's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Insidious
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Conjuring clicked for you, Insidious (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Conjuring clicked for you, The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Conjuring?
faq.answer: Try Insidious, The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It, The Black Phone—each shares DNA with The Conjuring while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Conjuring?
faq.answer: The Conjuring (2013) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Conjuring part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Conjuring Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
